title: Post-Semana Santa Security Transition: CJNG Violence Window Opens as Deployments Draw Down
detail: Day 50 since El Mencho's killing. Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026 officially concluded on April 12, ending the 16-day enhanced security deployment of 50,000+ National Guard elements across Mexico. With the drawdown now complete, Mexican security and intelligence agencies are on heightened alert for CJNG territorial opportunism — particularly from the El Jardinero/El Sapo faction, which declared open war on O3 and Los Cuinis on April 10–11 and immediately tested the security transition with the April 12 Zacatecas police attack. Security analysts assessed the Semana Santa period (252 violent incidents across 20 states, zero major incidents in tourist zones) as a tactical success for President Sheinbaum's government, but warn that CJNG's three-faction fracture — O3/Grupo Élite, El Jardinero/Zacatecas-Jalisco regional, and El Sapo/Puerto Vallarta port logistics — creates multiple simultaneous flashpoints as the enforcement pressure normalizes. Mexican forces maintained posture in Guadalajara's World Cup security corridor, now 59 days from the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match on June 11.

media.caption: InSight Crime analysis of CJNG post-Mencho trajectory

title: Rancho Izaguirre: FGR Continues DNA Analysis as Collectives Describe 'Hundreds' of Victims
detail: Search and identification work at Rancho Izaguirre CJNG training camp in Teuchitlán, Jalisco continued on April 13. FGR forensic teams processed calcined remains recovered from a septic tank discovered the previous day. Members of the Luz de Esperanza searching collective stated the scale of remains at the site suggests 'hundreds' of victims, not dozens — a claim officials declined to confirm pending analysis. The site has now yielded human remains spanning more than a decade, consistent with CJNG using the ranch as an extermination site from at least 2014 onwards. Oaxaca state prosecutors separately reported on April 13 that organized crime groups including CJNG-aligned cells are actively recruiting minors as young as 13 years old for homicide and extortion roles — highlighting the organization's systematic use of forced youth recruitment that has fueled its expansion despite sustained decapitation pressure.

media.caption: FGR continues DNA analysis at Rancho Izaguirre CJNG training camp
